brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer 'No' with precise enumeration of 4 cross-boundary dependencies and root cause analysis of the dependency cycle; multi-level analysis traces cycle from class level to module level to application boundary with second-order effects on compile-time imports; no explicit self-monitoring, bias-naming, or mid-analysis course-correction visible; verifies by concretely enumerating all cross-boundary edges in a table but no counterfactual or boundary condition test; presents the compile-time import assumption (runtime instantiation requires import) without flagging it as an assumption; mentions the interface/abstraction alternative at the end but does not evaluate it in depth; full step-by-step derivation from graph mapping through extraction to verdict is reproducible by a third party; correctly treats the C1→C3→C5→C1 cycle as a structural invariant rather than an error to eliminate, avoiding the anti-pattern of forcing well-foundedness; avoids both suppressed modes — no artificial well-foundedness imposed, no topology discontinuity introduced; follows S1 (scan) and S2 (classify) steps of the topology but does not traverse the branching gates, QUARANTINE, FREEFORM, or RE-ENTER nodes explicitly.
